q.en: What was the Alamo originally built as in the 1720s?
q.zh: 阿拉莫在18世纪20年代最初是作为什么建成的？

options.en: A Catholic mission and chapel
options.zh: 一座天主教传教站和小教堂

options.en: A military fort from the start
options.zh: 一开始就作为军事堡垒

options.en: A public school
A royal palace

explain.en: The book says the Alamo was built in the 1720s as one of five Spanish missions and was originally called Mission San Antonio de Valero.
explain.zh: 书中说阿拉莫建于18世纪20年代，是西班牙人建立的五座传教站之一，最初名为圣安东尼奥·德·瓦莱罗传教站。

q.en: What does the Spanish word "Alamo" mean?
q.zh: 西班牙语单词“Alamo”是什么意思？

options.en: Cottonwood
Chapel
Fortress
Mission

explain.en: Soldiers renamed the old mission the Alamo because Alamo is the Spanish word for cottonwood, and cottonwood trees grew wild nearby.
explain.zh: 士兵们把旧传教站改名为阿拉莫，因为“Alamo”在西班牙语中是棉白杨的意思，而附近野生长着棉白杨。

q.en: Who took command of the Alamo after Colonel Neill left in mid-February?
q.zh: 二月中旬尼尔上校离开后，谁接手了阿拉莫的指挥权？

options.en: William Barret Travis
James Bowie
David Crockett
Sam Houston

explain.en: Colonel Neill left to care for a sick family member and turned command over to William Barret Travis before departing.
explain.zh: 尼尔上校因家人患病离开，临走前把指挥权交给了威廉·巴雷特·特拉维斯。

q.en: In the glossary, what does the word "compound" mean?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，“compound”一词是什么意思？

options.en: A walled-off area containing a group of buildings
options.zh: 由围墙圈起、包含一组建筑的区域

options.en: An army of ordinary citizens
options.zh: 由普通公民组成的军队

options.en: A place where Christians spread their faith
options.zh: 基督徒传播信仰的地方

options.en: A tactic of surrounding a fort

explain.en: The glossary defines compound as 'a walled-off area containing a group of buildings,' matching how the mission buildings surrounded a large compound.
explain.zh: 词汇表将 compound 定义为“由围墙圈起、包含一组建筑的区域”，与传教站建筑围出一个大院子的描述一致。

q.en: How long did the Battle of the Alamo last?
q.zh: 阿拉莫战役持续了多长时间？

options.en: About 90 minutes
13 days
A full week
Three hours

explain.en: The book states that when the sun rose, the Alamo was quiet and the battle had lasted just 90 minutes.
explain.zh: 书中说太阳升起时阿拉莫一片寂静，战斗仅持续了90分钟。

q.en: Why does the author include a map of the United States and Mexico in the book?
q.zh: 作者为什么在书中放入一张美国和墨西哥的地图？

options.en: To show where the defenders are buried
options.zh: 为了显示守军埋葬的地点

options.en: To help readers understand the geography of Texas and Mexico around 1833
options.zh: 帮助读者了解1833年前后得克萨斯和墨西哥的地理状况

options.en: To illustrate the inside layout of the Alamo
options.zh: 为了展示阿拉莫的内部布局

options.en: To list the Mexican army's supply routes
options.zh: 为了列出墨西哥军队的补给路线

explain.en: The map is labeled 'Map of United States and Mexico circa 1833' and helps readers picture where Texas and the disputed area were located.
explain.zh: 地图标注为“约1833年的美国和墨西哥地图”，帮助读者了解得克萨斯和有争议地区的地理位置。

q.en: What can you conclude about why Sam Houston retreated eastward instead of fighting Santa Anna right away?
q.zh: 关于萨姆·休斯顿为什么向东撤退而不立即与圣安娜开战，你能得出什么结论？

options.en: He had no soldiers at all
He wanted to protect his army and wait for the right moment to attack
options.zh: 他想保全自己的军队，等待合适的进攻时机

options.en: He planned to surrender to Mexico
He was ordered to by Santa Anna
options.zh: 他接到了圣安娜的命令

explain.en: Houston knew losing his army would end the revolution, so he slowly retreated and later launched a surprise attack at San Jacinto.
explain.zh: 休斯顿知道失去军队就意味着革命失败，所以他缓慢东撤，后来在圣哈辛托发起突袭。

q.en: About how many of Santa Anna's soldiers were dead or wounded at the Alamo?
q.zh: 在阿拉莫，圣安娜的士兵大约有多少伤亡？

options.en: About 189
About 250
About 600
About 6,000

explain.en: The book says about 600 of Santa Anna's soldiers were dead or wounded, even though he had triumphed.
explain.zh: 书中说尽管圣安娜获胜，但他约有600名士兵死伤。

q.en: Why does the author include the 'Remember the Alamo' section at the end of the book?
q.zh: 作者为什么在书末加入“铭记阿拉莫”这一节？

options.en: To explain how the Alamo is preserved and honored today
options.zh: 为了说明阿拉莫如今如何被保存和纪念

options.en: To describe the battle tactics used in 1836
options.zh: 为了描述1836年使用的作战战术

options.en: To list the names of all the defenders
options.zh: 为了列出所有守军的姓名

options.en: To argue that Mexico was in the right
options.zh: 为了论证墨西哥一方是对的

explain.en: This section shows the Alamo was later repaired, acquired by Texas, and maintained as a historic shrine by the Daughters of the Republic of Texas.
explain.zh: 这一节说明阿拉莫后来被修缮、由得克萨斯州接管，并由得克萨斯共和国之女组织作为历史圣地维护。

q.en: Based on the text, what was a main cause of the Texas Revolution?
q.zh: 根据文章，得克萨斯革命的主要原因是什么？

options.en: Texians simply wanted cheaper land and nothing more
options.zh: 得克萨斯人只想要更便宜的土地，别无他求

options.en: Mexico feared Texas would break free and restricted settlement, causing hostility
options.zh: 墨西哥担心得克萨斯独立并限制移民，从而引发敌对

options.en: Santa Anna was elected president of Mexico
options.zh: 圣安娜当选墨西哥总统

options.en: The Alamo had already been destroyed

explain.en: As more Texians arrived, Mexico tried to stop settlement in 1830, which caused hostility between Texians and Mexican troops and led to revolt.
explain.zh: 随着得克萨斯人增多，墨西哥在1830年试图阻止移民，导致得克萨斯人与墨西哥军队敌对并最终反抗。

prompt.en: If you had been a Texian leader in 1836, what would you have done differently to defend the Alamo, and why?
prompt.zh: 如果你是1836年的一位得克萨斯领导人，你会采取什么不同的办法来保卫阿拉莫？为什么？

en: Think about Travis's request for help that never arrived and the poor condition of the old fort.
Consider Santa Anna's much larger army and the hard choices the defenders faced.
zh: 想一想特拉维斯请求支援却始终没有等到，以及这座旧堡垒当时的破败状况。
考虑圣安娜庞大的军队，以及守军所面临的艰难抉择。
